title: zanthoxylum caribaeum subsp. rugosum
intro: Zanthoxylum caribaeum subsp. rugosum, also known as Wild Lime or Mexican Prickly Ash, is a tropical plant species belonging to the Rutaceae family, characterized by its thorny branches and fragrant leaves. The plant's medicinal properties include its ability to act as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal agent, making it useful in treating wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Zanthoxylum caribaeum subsp. rugosum include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, boost the immune system, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark, leaves, and roots are often used to create teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
